channel.title: Trig on Zymacs
channel.description: Recent content in Trig on Zymacs

item.title: What is Cos
item.description:
Similar posts: What is Sin, What is PI, Why is the Pythogrean Theorem what it is?

Why would anyone want to know what Cos is? If you are asking that question? If you are asking that question then ask yourself why you clicked the link. Curiosity right? Well that ain’t why I wrote this. But it partly is.

PI: The constant that is the ratio for any circle’s circumference to its diameter. Try it out. Make a perfect circle however you will. It can be any size. Get its circumference and divide that by its diameter. The result will be the same regardless of what the circle radius will be. The scale factor relating change in diameter with change in circumference is a constant , PI.
